properties.name: West African Coast
properties.caption: Yellow fever is a mosquito-borne viral disease with African roots; Atlantic shipping carried infected people and vector conditions toward the Americas.
properties.mechanism: Warm ports, stored water, Aedes mosquitoes, people, and non-human primate cycles made this a vector ecology shaped by maritime movement.

claims.claim: Yellow fever is a mosquito-borne viral disease with African roots; Atlantic shipping carried infected people and vector conditions toward the Americas.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: West Africa / Malarial Fevers
properties.caption: Malaria is a parasitic disease; this route follows tropical mosquito ecology before reaching Caribbean waters.
properties.mechanism: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted by infected Anopheles mosquitoes. Warm climates, water storage, and uneven immunity shape risk.

claims.claim: Malaria is a parasitic disease; this route follows tropical mosquito ecology before reaching Caribbean waters.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: East African / camel-linked orthopox hypothesis
properties.caption: One molecular-origin model places variola emergence in eastern Africa 3,000 to 4,000 years ago and treats camel movement as a possible trigger within a debated source ecology.
properties.mechanism: Smallpox was caused by variola virus, an Orthopoxvirus. Camel-linked origin remains a hypothesis in the literature, so this point marks a debated source ecology.
properties.uncertainty_note: Camel-linked variola emergence is a model in the literature, not a settled origin claim.

claims.claim: One molecular-origin model places variola emergence in eastern Africa 3,000 to 4,000 years ago and treats camel movement as a possible trigger within a debated source ecology.
claims.notes: Camel-linked variola emergence is a model in the literature, not a settled origin claim.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Cattle / rinderpest ancestry
properties.caption: Measles is generally treated as a human morbillivirus related to rinderpest, with emergence tied to dense human-cattle settlement.
properties.mechanism: The closest known relative of measles virus was rinderpest virus. A 2020 molecular-clock study supports possible divergence as early as the 6th century BCE.
properties.uncertainty_note: The rinderpest relationship is well supported, but the divergence date remains model-dependent.

claims.claim: Measles is generally treated as a human morbillivirus related to rinderpest, with emergence tied to dense human-cattle settlement.
claims.notes: The rinderpest relationship is well supported, but the divergence date remains model-dependent.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Saint-Domingue / Caribbean Fever Ecology
properties.caption: The Caribbean shows yellow fever as port-and-plantation ecology: mosquitoes, stored water, dense movement, and non-immune arrivals.
properties.mechanism: Ships carried infected people, Aedes mosquitoes, water containers, and port conditions into places where yellow fever transmission could restart.

claims.claim: The Caribbean shows yellow fever as port-and-plantation ecology: mosquitoes, stored water, dense movement, and non-immune arrivals.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Philadelphia, 1793
properties.caption: A port-city endpoint where ship movement, refugees, summer heat, urban density, and mosquito habitat converged.
properties.mechanism: Maritime disease ecology reached beyond the dock.

claims.claim: A port-city endpoint where ship movement, refugees, summer heat, urban density, and mosquito habitat converged.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: HMS Salisbury
properties.caption: James Lind's 1747 scurvy trial gives the scurvy route a concrete shipboard case.
properties.mechanism: Scurvy is a logistics disease: time at sea, preserved food, and missing vitamin C.

claims.claim: James Lind's 1747 scurvy trial gives the scurvy route a concrete shipboard case.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: North Atlantic Crossing
properties.caption: The open ocean turns provisioning into a clock. The longer the crossing, the more the ship's diet becomes a biological trap.
properties.mechanism: A route between fresh-food departure and deficiency arrival.

claims.claim: The open ocean turns provisioning into a clock. The longer the crossing, the more the ship's diet becomes a biological trap.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Nassau, New Providence
properties.caption: The pirate republic sits inside a wider port network where epidemiological risk accumulates.
properties.mechanism: Pirate crews moved through places where food, water, mosquito vectors, infected people, injuries, and provisions mixed.

claims.claim: The pirate republic sits inside a wider port network where epidemiological risk accumulates.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Port Royal, Jamaica
properties.caption: A tropical port is an ecological exchange point with pirate history around it.
properties.mechanism: Sailors, merchants, enslaved people, stored water, mosquitoes, wounds, alcohol, and provisions overlapped.

claims.claim: A tropical port is an ecological exchange point with pirate history around it.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Tortuga
properties.caption: Island bases let crews reprovision, recruit, recover, and carry risk into the next voyage.
properties.mechanism: Pirate geography matters because ports reset the ship ecology.

claims.claim: Island bases let crews reprovision, recruit, recover, and carry risk into the next voyage.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: London / Naval Hospitals
properties.caption: A practical hygiene point: bathing, clean clothes, delousing, ventilation, and bedding.
properties.mechanism: Epidemic typhus is caused by Rickettsia prowazekii and spread by infected body lice, especially where people cannot bathe or change clothes regularly.

claims.claim: A practical hygiene point: bathing, clean clothes, delousing, ventilation, and bedding.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Below Deck
properties.caption: This is the shipboard disease machine: bunks, bedding, clothing, lice, bad air, dirty water, and no escape from exposure.
properties.mechanism: Typhus, flux, and other shipboard illnesses shared this confined environment, but they moved through different mechanisms: lice, contaminated food or water, wounds, and respiratory spread.

claims.claim: This is the shipboard disease machine: bunks, bedding, clothing, lice, bad air, dirty water, and no escape from exposure.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Port Arrival / Quarantine
properties.caption: Smallpox and measles move through close quarters aboard ship, then into susceptible populations ashore.
properties.mechanism: Voyage length, isolation, bedding, clothes, and port quarantine shaped whether measles virus or variola virus cases burned out aboard ship or spread after arrival.

claims.claim: Smallpox and measles move through close quarters aboard ship, then into susceptible populations ashore.
claims.notes: Port-arrival transmission is supported generally; exact voyage outcomes vary by immunity and quarantine practice.

properties.uncertainty_note: Port-arrival transmission is supported generally; exact voyage outcomes vary by immunity and quarantine practice.
properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Smallpox Hospital Ships
properties.caption: Hospital hulks make the port-health problem visible: ships could isolate disease as well as transport it.
properties.mechanism: Smallpox quarantine and isolation were maritime infrastructures as much as medical decisions.

claims.claim: Hospital hulks make the port-health problem visible: ships could isolate disease as well as transport it.
claims.notes: This supports maritime quarantine infrastructure, not the contested deep-origin hypothesis.

properties.uncertainty_note: This supports maritime quarantine infrastructure, not the contested deep-origin hypothesis.
properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Ship Surgery / Wound Care
properties.caption: Injury became infectious inside the ship environment.
properties.mechanism: Cuts, fractures, stab wounds, burns, unwashed tools, dirty bandages, bedding, and delayed care created infection risk. Sepsis is the dangerous systemic response to severe infection.

claims.claim: Injury became infectious inside the ship environment.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Water Cask / Latrine Boundary
properties.caption: Contaminated food and water formed the route of exposure.
properties.mechanism: Flux and dysentery are syndromic labels. Bacteria such as Shigella, and sometimes protozoal parasites, spread through fecally contaminated water, food, hands, and surfaces.

claims.claim: Contaminated food and water formed the route of exposure.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Food and Water Contamination
properties.caption: Typhoid fever sits beside flux: contaminated food and water, overlapping symptoms, and hygiene interventions before germ theory.
properties.mechanism: Typhoid fever is caused by Salmonella Typhi. Water casks, hands, food, and fecal contamination define this food-water ecology.

claims.claim: Typhoid fever sits beside flux: contaminated food and water, overlapping symptoms, and hygiene interventions before germ theory.
claims.notes: Feature-level provenance for displayed map text.

properties.route_note: Mapped geometry is schematic unless the claim_type says documented route or port location.

properties.name: Grosse Île Quarantine Station
properties.caption: A later but concrete North Atlantic quarantine endpoint for fever ships, crowding, and body-louse risk.
properties.mechanism: Ship fever was epidemic typhus: Rickettsia prowazekii transmitted by infected body lice in conditions of crowding and poor hygiene.

claims.claim: A later but concrete North Atlantic quarantine endpoint for fever ships, crowding, and body-louse risk.
